---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "conversion"
namespace: "Workflows/ingestion"
description: "A pipeline to convert .mtx, .h5 or .h5ad file formats to .h5mu."
type: "workflow"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `conversion`  
Namespace: `workflows/ingestion`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/workflows/ingestion/conversion)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/workflows/ingestion/conversion)

:::



## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/conversion/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/workflows/ingestion/conversion).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/conversion/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name           |Description                                                                                     |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--id`         |ID of the sample.                                                                               |`string`, required, example: `"foo"`      |
|`--input`      |Path to the sample to be converted. Possible format options include:   - .h5ad   - .h5   - .mtx |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5ad"` |
|`--input_type` |Type of the input file                                                                          |`string`, required                        |


### Outputs

|Name       |Description                            |Attributes                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output` |Name or template for the output files. |`file`, example: `"output.h5mu"` |


### Conversion from h5ad

|Name         |Description                                                       |Attributes                            |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--modality` |Name of the modality where the h5ad is stored in the h5mu object. |List of `string`, multiple_sep: `";"` |

## Authors


  * Dries Schaumont [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/DriesSchaumont) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4389-0440) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-schaumont) (author, maintainer)

  * Dries De Maeyer [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/ddemaeyer) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-de-maeyer-b46a814) (author)

## Visualisation

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    v20["from_10xh5_to_h5mu"]
    v56["from_h5ad_to_h5mu"]
    v92["from_10xmtx_to_h5mu"]
    style v20 fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style v56 fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style v92 fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
```
